jokes.text: Liz, women wearing men's watches is so over. The new thing is to get an Adam's apple.
jokes.setup: Fashion advice about women wearing men's watches
jokes.punchline: Suggesting getting an Adam's apple as the new trend
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna absurdist vanity - treating surgical masculinization as a fashion trend is perfectly in character and genuinely surprising

jokes.text: My mom just had this fixed for me. It was my grandfather's. He worked for years at Union Station in D.C. As a pickpocket.
jokes.setup: Sentimental story about grandfather's watch repair
jokes.punchline: Grandfather was a pickpocket, not an honorable railroad worker
jokes.explanation: Strong misdirection with perfect timing - Union Station setup makes you think legitimate work, then subverts completely

jokes.text: What is this, Jabba's pleasure skiff?
jokes.setup: Crew gambling operation looks sketchy
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to Star Wars criminal enterprise
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but fairly predictable - crew does look like criminals, Star Wars reference fits but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Get a tub of New suits from the suit emporium. Kabletown guys notice. Your hair grows back. Everything grows back.
jokes.setup: Benefits of winning the lottery money
jokes.punchline: Escalating to magical hair/everything regeneration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from practical benefits to absurd magical thinking - fits the working-class superstition angle

jokes.text: That game is not for softhands and faceworkers. It's for strongs and lifters.
jokes.setup: Warning Liz against joining crew lottery
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's made-up class system terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth-ism - creating his own pseudo-academic terminology for blue collar vs. white collar divide with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Your boos are not scaring me. I know most of you are not ghosts.
jokes.setup: Crew booing Liz for joining their lottery
jokes.punchline: Taking 'boos' literally as ghost sounds
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literal interpretation joke - 'most of you are not ghosts' implies careful consideration of each crew member's mortality status

jokes.text: How did you know that? You're like rain man. Quick, how many toothpicks are on the ground? - Zero. - You need to go to Vegas.
jokes.setup: Liz calculated 1 in 52 odds, crew thinks she's savant
jokes.punchline: Zero toothpicks on ground, but crew still impressed
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion of Rain Man trope - obvious answer but crew's continued amazement sells the bit

jokes.text: I said time to end the charade and adjust my schedule to buy a new vase.
jokes.setup: Toofer in punishment corner for unknown offense
jokes.punchline: His crime was pronouncing 'schedule' pretentiously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toofer characterization - his pretentious pronunciation getting him literally punished by the writers

jokes.text: Schedule. Ha! Stay in the corner.
jokes.setup: Toofer looking up correct pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Even correct pronunciation doesn't save him
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback - writers' arbitrary cruelty to Toofer regardless of his correctness

jokes.text: America. Whiskey. Liberal.
jokes.setup: Random pronunciation website with Jack's voice
jokes.punchline: Words perfectly represent Jack's worldview
jokes.explanation: Efficient character comedy - three words that perfectly encapsulate Jack's conservative businessman identity

jokes.text: Lemon...lesbian...Frankenstein... wants her shoes back.
jokes.setup: More words from Jack's pronunciation recordings
jokes.punchline: Accidentally forms insult that sounds like Jack talking about Liz
jokes.explanation: Exceptional joke - random dictionary words accidentally form Jack's typical Liz insult pattern, perfect character integration

jokes.text: Just out of frame is a wheelbarrow full of cocaine.
jokes.setup: Jack nostalgic about 1985 GE quarterly report cover
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning 80s cocaine excess
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about 80s corporate culture - delivered with Jack's casual corporate nostalgia

jokes.text: So why the trip down memory lame? Ha ha! High-fiving a million angels.
jokes.setup: Liz asking why Jack is reminiscing
jokes.punchline: Memory lane becomes 'memory lame' plus bizarre celebration
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but the 'high-fiving angels' feels random rather than character-driven

jokes.text: And a G.E. woman for one week of corporate espionage at Revlon.
jokes.setup: Jack's 25 years as a GE man
jokes.punchline: Brief stint in drag for corporate espionage
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jack character moment - casually revealing extreme corporate dedication through cross-dressing espionage

jokes.text: They wanted me to record every word of the dictionary to preserve the perfect American accent in case of nuclear war.
jokes.setup: Explaining how his voice ended up on pronunciation website
jokes.punchline: Cold War era plan to preserve accents post-apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist premise - treating accent preservation as Cold War priority is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: Oh, the things it's been dragged into. Thomas the tank engine. Wu-Tang songs. ♪ Ah, yeah, just like that ♪ ♪ make 'em clap, make 'em clap ♪
jokes.setup: Jack's voice being used in inappropriate contexts
jokes.punchline: From children's trains to explicit rap music
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and Jack singing hip-hop is inherently funny given his character

jokes.text: Huh, I always forget you used to be poor.
jokes.setup: Jack talking about having to work
jokes.punchline: Liz's tone-deaf response to his hardship
jokes.explanation: Good Liz character moment - her social awkwardness manifesting as inappropriate casualness about class differences

jokes.text: It began when my father left and I started working on the Boston docks as a 12-year-old stevedore. Bales up, you micks! Bales up!
jokes.setup: Jack's childhood work history
jokes.punchline: 12-year-old using ethnic slurs as dock supervisor
jokes.explanation: Dark character comedy - child labor made worse by Jack's precocious bigotry and authority

jokes.text: It wasn't the feces that got to you, Lemon. It was the crudely scrawled notes of 'help me.'
jokes.setup: Jack worked at Princeton monkey lab
jokes.punchline: Monkeys were writing desperate pleas for help
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark escalation - implies both monkey intelligence and Jack's callousness to suffering

jokes.text: The fall of 1988. A young Liz Lemon enters the university of Maryland. Richard Marx haircut. Pilonidal cyst under control.
jokes.setup: Jack narrating Liz's college story dramatically
jokes.punchline: Specific 80s details and gross medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good character details - Richard Marx haircut dates it perfectly, pilonidal cyst is gross and specific to Liz

jokes.text: Don't worry about getting to your point. I'm going to live forever.
jokes.setup: Liz starting to tell a long story
jokes.punchline: Jack's sarcastic acceptance of eternal listening
jokes.explanation: Good Jack sarcasm but fairly standard 'long story' complaint structure

jokes.text: A blonde girl high-fived me.
jokes.setup: Liz describing her brief popularity in college
jokes.punchline: High-five from blonde as peak social achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - treating minimal social contact as profound validation shows her social anxiety

jokes.text: Paula's nickname for my penis.
jokes.setup: Drawing the four of clubs from lottery
jokes.punchline: Immediately oversharing sexual nickname
jokes.explanation: Standard TMI humor - gets uncomfortable laugh but not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: Wait, for real? - You know it, Arriflex.
jokes.setup: Liz offering to buy drinks for crew
jokes.punchline: Crew member's nickname is camera equipment brand
jokes.explanation: Good industry-specific nickname humor - Arriflex cameras are actually used in TV production

jokes.text: Well, on some level, yeah. That's a four. That's a nine.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if she can make sense of financial numbers
jokes.punchline: Liz identifying basic numerals instead of understanding data
jokes.explanation: Great Liz character moment - her literal-mindedness and complete innumeracy played for perfect deadpan comedy

jokes.text: What about three years ago when I said there should be more TV shows about cake?
jokes.setup: Jack calling her previous comment the smartest thing she's said
jokes.punchline: Her other 'smart' idea was cake TV shows
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific callback - perfectly captures Liz's food obsession and low intellectual bar

jokes.text: They'll probably give me a crystal plaque, and I in turn will reward one of them with a name remembrance.
jokes.setup: Jack planning to visit successful microwave team
jokes.punchline: Treating remembering someone's name as a gift
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Jack character moment - his casual dehumanization of subordinates treated as corporate generosity

jokes.text: Oh, Lemon, please. Money can't buy happiness. It is happiness.
jokes.setup: Standard 'money can't buy happiness' setup
jokes.punchline: Jack's correction that money IS happiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack philosophy - subverts common saying with his materialistic worldview in most efficient way possible

jokes.text: I love unicorns.
jokes.setup: Drunk crew members after Liz's generosity
jokes.punchline: Random emotional declaration about unicorns
jokes.explanation: Weak drunk humor - random = funny approach without much craft, though brief delivery helps

jokes.text: Obama is very presidential.
jokes.setup: Crew making Jack's voice say anything
jokes.punchline: Conservative Jack forced to compliment Obama
jokes.explanation: Good character-based irony - Jack's voice saying things he'd never agree with

jokes.text: When some of us had to spend their freshman year making those recordings. And leading a disastrous monkey escape.
jokes.setup: Jack criticizing crew for acting like college freshmen
jokes.punchline: Reveals he caused monkey lab disaster during his freshman year
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalation - Jack's college job led to institutional disaster

jokes.text: Say Hey, Willie mays.
jokes.setup: Crew member trying pronunciation website
jokes.punchline: Using Willie Mays' nickname as command
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay - connection between 'say hey' and website command isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: You know, usually everyone around here makes me feel like Hitler. But today I feel like Hitler in Germany.
jokes.setup: Liz describing her usual unpopularity
jokes.punchline: Feeling popular like Hitler did in Nazi Germany
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Liz's tone-deaf Nazi comparison for feeling popular is perfectly inappropriate and funny

jokes.text: Wow, I would experiment with that girl. - Too small. - That's me for two weeks in college.
jokes.setup: Jenna commenting on attractive woman, someone says too small
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're talking about college-age Liz
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing they're looking at Liz's college photo, with typical Jenna sexual commentary

jokes.text: You probably said 'fortnight.'
jokes.setup: Liz wondering what ended her college popularity
jokes.punchline: Jenna correctly identifying pretentious vocabulary as the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jenna intuitively understanding social dynamics and Liz's verbal pretensions

jokes.text: I'm the hot blonde. And I'm the nerd who takes off his glasses and everyone realizes he's handsome. And you're the R.A.
jokes.setup: Everyone has their role in the workplace dynamic
jokes.punchline: Casting Liz as the rule-enforcing authority figure
jokes.explanation: Good character typing - Jenna as hot blonde is obvious, Lutz's delusion is funny, Liz as RA is perfect

jokes.text: Only if R.A. stands for 'really awesome.'
jokes.setup: Being called the R.A. (residential advisor)
jokes.punchline: Liz's desperate reinterpretation of the acronym
jokes.explanation: Standard desperate reinterpretation joke - gets point across about Liz's denial but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Oh, okay, look. What happened was Suzanne from ad sales got pushed into me in the elevator. I didn't try to touch her. And the sort of 'unh' sound I made was about something else.
jokes.setup: Jack calling Pete unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Pete immediately confessing to workplace inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Pete's guilty conscience making him confess to unrelated harassment incident

jokes.text: You know, whenever I tell my wife a work story, she pointed this out the other day... I always... always smile a little when I'm talking about you.
jokes.setup: Jack asking Pete to be his friend
jokes.punchline: Pete's overly intimate confession about feelings
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Pete's earnest emotional oversharing in response to casual friendship request

jokes.text: ♪ Rollin' with my homie, me and Jackie d. ♪ ♪ Bitches get ready for a sex party ♪
jokes.setup: Jack asking Pete to freestyle rap
jokes.punchline: Pete's awkward white-guy rap with explicit content
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward white rap trope but Pete's eagerness to please Jack makes it character-specific

jokes.text: 'cause it's too much fun.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they don't play football in the studio
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic reason after someone gets hurt
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcastic response. Shows Liz's dry humor but nothing exceptional.

jokes.text: What? It's me, the lizard. You can start calling me that. Look, we appreciate what you did for the crew last night, but you left some people out and that's not cool. What are you talking about? The Blizzard wouldn't do that. That's another option.
jokes.setup: Liz trying out new nicknames with the crew
jokes.punchline: Cycling through increasingly desperate nickname options
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Liz's desperate attempts at coolness through self-imposed nicknames

jokes.text: And who knows what it's gonna be? - Only the blizbian knows.
jokes.setup: Liz promising surprise for alcoholic crew members
jokes.punchline: Creating 'Blizbian' portmanteau nickname
jokes.explanation: Continues Liz's nickname desperation with increasingly awkward portmanteau attempts

jokes.text: This is why I hated my first two weeks at the royal Tampa academy of dramatic tricks. No one knew who was the sluttiest. But I showed them. Oh, I showed them all. And when we graduated a week later...
jokes.setup: Jenna relating to unclear social dynamics
jokes.punchline: Three-week drama school where she established sexual dominance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - fake drama school with competitive sluttiness is exactly her character

jokes.text: Look, there is no cool Liz. There's only R.A. Liz. You're wrong. My glasses are dirty. Ogbert?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Liz's identity
jokes.punchline: Toofer proving the nerd stereotype by cleaning glasses
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that reinforces character types. Well-executed callback to earlier nerd reference.

jokes.text: Sir, this lab requires clearance. Clearance? I'm your boss. I'm Jack Donaghy. I'm sorry, your names all sound the same to us, John Donovan.
jokes.setup: Engineer telling Jack he needs clearance for his own lab
jokes.punchline: Reversing racist name confusion stereotype on Jack
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal of racist trope - Indian engineers treating white executives as indistinguishable is brilliant social commentary

jokes.text: Trivection? What is this, 2009? The future is the tk-421. A new model? How many vections does it have? Five.
jokes.setup: Jack's pride in trivection oven technology
jokes.punchline: Five-vection technology making trivection obsolete
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of nonsense technology terms - 'vections' becoming a measurable unit is absurd but consistent

jokes.text: Popcorn setting medium.
jokes.setup: New microwave's voice feature
jokes.punchline: It's using Jack's recorded voice
jokes.explanation: Strong callback - Jack's voice being used in the very product he's trying to stop is perfect irony

jokes.text: Well, which time?
jokes.setup: Jack threatening they'll wish they'd never been born
jokes.punchline: Implying multiple births/reincarnations
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response treating reincarnation casually while deflating Jack's threat

jokes.text: You know my uncle was a tinkerer. Until the FBI shot him.
jokes.setup: Kenneth commenting on Jack tinkering with microwave
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of uncle's violent death by FBI
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kenneth dark humor - casual mention of FBI assassination suggests his family's criminal/subversive history

jokes.text: But after I left Kentucky Mountain Bible College, it still kept going. Until it was shut down because of the wolves.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's philosophy about letting go of special places
jokes.punchline: His Bible college was eventually destroyed by wolves
jokes.explanation: Outstanding Kenneth moment - perfectly absurd backstory detail that explains nothing while being completely in character

jokes.text: Television studies with a minor in Bible sexuality.
jokes.setup: Jack asking about Kenneth's college major
jokes.punchline: Combining TV studies with biblical sexuality minor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth academic background - combining his TV obsession with his repressed religious sexuality

jokes.text: Damn it, it's supposed to do that.
jokes.setup: Kenneth gets shocked by microwave Jack is trying to prove is defective
jokes.punchline: Jack realizing the 'defect' is actually a safety feature
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Jack's sabotage attempts keep proving the microwave works correctly

jokes.text: I don't even care that we've taken a 73-minute break to eat it.
jokes.setup: Crew enjoying ice cream Liz bought them
jokes.punchline: Absurdly long break time for ice cream
jokes.explanation: Decent workplace comedy - specific time duration makes the work shirking more absurd

jokes.text: Hey, somebody brought a dog to work. Oh, and it definitely doesn't have any of its own waste on its feet... Oh!
jokes.setup: Excited about dog at work
jokes.punchline: Immediate discovery that dog has feces on feet
jokes.explanation: Basic gross-out humor with predictable setup and payoff - gets reaction but lacks craft

jokes.text: I totally forgot, Broseph. You're a lactose-intolerant alcoholic.
jokes.setup: Tony can't enjoy bar or ice cream treats
jokes.punchline: Combining two conditions that exclude him from both treats
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - specific combination of conditions that creates workplace social isolation

jokes.text: Lizard, lizard, lizard! Lizard, lizard! Lizard! Lizard! Lizard!
jokes.setup: Crew chanting Liz's chosen nickname
jokes.punchline: Mass enthusiasm for her desperate nickname choice
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing crew's manipulation of Liz's desperation for acceptance

jokes.text: Make sure you wear one of those Mexican ponchos.
jokes.setup: Jack planning college hangout with Pete
jokes.punchline: Specific request for stereotypical stoner attire
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character moment - his idealized vision of college friendship includes cliched hippie costume

jokes.text: Why don't you ask Choctee, an inuit who wants a hot bowl of naglak... a man and food I just made up to illustrate a point?
jokes.setup: Jack creating hypothetical microwave user scenario
jokes.punchline: Admitting he invented the person and food to win argument
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - Jack's desperate argument construction revealed and abandoned mid-sentence

jokes.text: At least that's what my Nana is telling me from that tunnel of light behind you.
jokes.setup: Kenneth giving Jack advice about letting go
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning near-death experience/hallucination
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth moment - treating hypothermic hallucinations as normal conversation with dead relatives

jokes.text: Your Nana is an idiot.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's dying grandmother giving spiritual advice
jokes.punchline: Jack's casual dismissal of dead relative's wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack response - his complete inability to respect Kenneth's supernatural experiences

jokes.text: You know I'm not a delivery man. I'm wearing a suit and carrying a microwave.
jokes.setup: Restaurant workers assuming Jack is delivery man
jokes.punchline: Jack pointing out the obvious contradiction in his appearance
jokes.explanation: Good observation about class assumptions - suit-wearing executives don't typically deliver food

jokes.text: 1985. That's not a time. I guess it could be a year. The year I started working here, actually. That's interesting. 1985.
jokes.setup: Microwave displaying 1985 instead of cooking time
jokes.punchline: Jack realizing it's showing his career start year
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - microwave seems to be responding to Jack's emotional state about his career

jokes.text: You all sound the same to us, Jock Dungaree.
jokes.setup: Continuing the reversed racism theme
jokes.punchline: Even more ridiculous mangling of Jack's name
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalating the name confusion - 'Jock Dungaree' is even more ridiculous than 'John Donovan'

jokes.text: Goodbye. Potato. Goodbye.
jokes.setup: Microwave playing Jack's old voice recordings
jokes.punchline: Young Jack recorded random words including emotional potato farewell
jokes.explanation: Exceptional absurdist moment - young Jack saying goodbye to potato is perfectly random yet somehow poignant

jokes.text: Are we racist, or do those guys look a lot alike?
jokes.setup: Workers confusing different white executives
jokes.punchline: Self-aware question about their own potential racism
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the episode's racism reversal theme - characters questioning their own perceptions

jokes.text: She had overlapping pregnancies five months apart.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining why his college experience was cut short
jokes.punchline: Impossible biological timeline for pregnancies
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist biology - 'overlapping pregnancies' is medically impossible but delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: ♪ I don't know the words except park bench ♪
jokes.setup: Jack trying to play Aqualung on guitar
jokes.punchline: Admitting he only knows two words from the song
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jack's cultural gaps showing through failed attempt at classic rock

jokes.text: Not stolen property of Adolf Lemon?
jokes.setup: Checking the watch Liz gave Tony
jokes.punchline: Revealing it belonged to 'Adolf Lemon' - Liz's Nazi-named grandfather
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - 'Adolf Lemon' is perfect absurd Nazi family name that explains Liz's issues

jokes.text: And if I see that filthy dog again, I will put it down. I will put it down with a smile.
jokes.setup: Liz laying down workplace rules as the RA
jokes.punchline: Threatening to kill dog with enthusiastic sadism
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark escalation - Liz's authoritarian turn becoming genuinely threatening with the smile detail

jokes.text: I love you too. High-fiving a million angels.
jokes.setup: Emotional moment with Pete during college night
jokes.punchline: Jack's bizarre celebration phrase returning
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but the phrase works better in original context - feels forced here

jokes.text: Maybe what I see as red, you perceive as green.
jokes.setup: Jack having deep college-style philosophical discussion
jokes.punchline: Classic stoned dorm room philosophy question
jokes.explanation: Standard college philosophy cliche but works for Jack's character attempt at casual intellectualism

jokes.text: Oh, god, she means the pizza. No, she's unhinging her jaw!
jokes.setup: Liz offering to shotgun something
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding leads to horror at Liz's eating method
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with snake-like imagery - Liz's monstrous eating habits revealed through misdirection
